skill: Nonlinear equations in one variable and systems of equations in two variables
stem: (18p)/(6q) = 3√(r + 11). The given equation relates the distinct positive real numbers p, q, and r. Which equation correctly expresses r in terms of p and q?
rationale: Simplify the left side: 18p/(6q) = 3p/q. The equation becomes 3p/q = 3√(r + 11). Dividing both sides by 3 gives p/q = √(r + 11). Squaring (both sides are positive): (p/q)2 = r + 11. Subtracting 11 yields r = (p/q)2 − 11.
